source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a small bipropellant monopropellant-compatible titanium spacecraft propellant tank (fuel side) sized for 12 kg of monomethyl hydrazine (MMH), delivered as a multi-part assembly because the tank pressure boundary is formed by several spun / machined / welded families: (A) a cylindrical body shell, (B) two hemispherical end caps girth-welded to the body, (C) two machined outlet/inlet bosses bonded/welded into the poles, and (D) a 4-point skirt flange brazed at the cylindrical girth (thermally decoupled and out of the pressure boundary stress path). The governing standard is ECSS-E-ST-32-02C Rev.2 (11 December 2024). For qualification metallic hardware Table 6-2 prescribes proof pressure = 1.5 x MDP and burst pressure = 2.0 x MDP. The tank operates in a blow-down pressurization scheme with helium.

Geometry. Inner diameter 320 mm, cylindrical body length 200 mm, two hemispherical end caps of radius 160 mm; internal volume ~33.3 L. One 19 mm ID propellant outlet boss at one pole and one 12 mm ID pressurant inlet boss at the opposite pole. Tank wall thickness is a design variable bounded at 1.2 mm minimum.

Material. Ti-6Al-4V Grade 5 annealed, E = 113.8 GPa, nu = 0.342, rho = 4430 kg/m^3, Fty = 860 MPa, Ftu = 950 MPa, fracture toughness K_IC = 75 MPa*m^0.5. Weld efficiency E_w = 0.85 for longitudinal girth weld.

Operating envelope. NOP = 22 bar (2.2 MPa). MDP = 25.0 bar (2.5 MPa). Proof = 37.5 bar (3.75 MPa). Burst = 50 bar (5.0 MPa). Design life: 20 pressurization cycles + 4 proof cycles, safe-life factor 4x. Operating temperature -10 C to +50 C.

Load cases. LC1 (proof): internal 3.75 MPa, stress must not exceed Fty anywhere. LC2 (burst): internal 5.0 MPa. LC3 (launch quasi-static): MDP 2.5 MPa internal + 15 g axial + 8 g lateral on 12 kg propellant + 6 kg dry tank ~18 kg inertial at skirt. LC4 (leak-before-burst): semi-elliptical flaw a_i = 0.3 mm, 2c = 1.5 mm. LC5 (cyclic life): 20 service + 4x safe-life => 80 equivalent cycles.

Pass/fail criteria reproduce ECSS-E-ST-32-02C verbatim and are evaluated on the fully welded tank assembly. (R1) LC1 proof pressure: max von Mises <= Fty = 860 MPa. (R2) LC2 burst: max von Mises <= Ftu * E_w = 807.5 MPa in weld regions and Ftu = 950 MPa in parent material. (R3) LC3 combined MDP + launch: sigma <= Fty / 1.25 = 688 MPa AND sigma <= Ftu / 1.25 = 760 MPa. (R4) LC4 leak-before-burst: a_crit must exceed wall thickness. (R5) LC5 fatigue: Paris-law crack growth from 0.3 mm over 80 cycles must not reach a_crit. (R6) Dry tank mass <= 7.5 kg (entire assembly including skirt).

Assembly policy. The pressure boundary is formed by the body shell + end caps + bosses interconnected by girth welds whose efficiency factor E_w applies only to weld regions. Therefore every pass/fail is evaluated on the integrated welded assembly; no part is valid standalone because the weld stress-concentration and leak-before-burst checks are inherently assembly-level.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary verification is axisymmetric or 3D linear-static pressure FEM for LC1-LC3 (TET10 or shell-of-revolution, 1 mm mesh in girth weld region). LC4 requires linear elastic fracture mechanics (Newman-Raju or J-integral FEM). LC5 requires Paris-law crack-growth integration. Buckling analysis under external collapse is NOT required. Vibration analysis is excluded. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are excluded.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of the complete assembly (body + caps + bosses + skirt), dry mass in kg, wall thickness in mm, max von Mises at proof / burst / launch in MPa, critical flaw depth at burst in mm, cycles-to-through-wall from Paris-law integration.
short_prompt:
Design a small titanium spacecraft propellant tank (fuel side) sized for 12 kg of MMH, delivered as a multi-part pressure-boundary assembly: cylindrical body shell + two hemispherical end caps (girth-welded) + two machined outlet/inlet bosses + a 4-point brazed skirt flange. Governing standard ECSS-E-ST-32-02C Rev.2 (11 December 2024): proof = 1.5 * MDP, burst = 2.0 * MDP.

Envelope. ID 320 mm, cylinder length 200 mm, two hemispherical caps R = 160 mm (volume ~33.3 L); 19 mm ID propellant outlet; 12 mm ID pressurant inlet; wall thickness >= 1.2 mm.

Material Ti-6Al-4V Grade 5 annealed (Fty = 860 MPa, Ftu = 950 MPa, E = 113.8 GPa, rho = 4430 kg/m^3, K_IC = 75 MPa*m^0.5). Weld efficiency E_w = 0.85.

Operating envelope: NOP 22 bar (2.2 MPa); MDP 25.0 bar (2.5 MPa); proof 37.5 bar (3.75 MPa); burst 50 bar (5.0 MPa); 20 cycles + 4 proof, 4x safe-life (80 equivalent); -10 C to +50 C.

Load cases.
- LC1 proof: internal 3.75 MPa.
- LC2 burst: internal 5.0 MPa.
- LC3 launch: MDP 2.5 MPa + 15 g axial + 8 g lateral on ~18 kg.
- LC4 leak-before-burst: a_i = 0.3 mm, 2c = 1.5 mm.
- LC5 fatigue: 80 equivalent cycles.

Dry mass <= 7.5 kg.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: LC4 leak-before-burst needs Newman-Raju or J-integral K-solution.
LC5 fatigue life from 0.3 mm initial flaw over 80 equivalent cycles.

prompt.title: Ti-6Al-4V spacecraft MMH propellant tank assembly per ECSS-E-ST-32-02C
prompt.brief: 33.3 L Ti-6Al-4V bipropellant tank with welded cylindrical body, 2 hemispherical caps, 2 bosses, brazed skirt; MDP 2.5 MPa, proof 1.5x, burst 2.0x.

parts.name: cylindrical_body_shell
parts.role: seamless or longitudinally-welded Ti cylinder forming the cylindrical pressure boundary

parts.name: hemispherical_end_cap
parts.role: spun hemispherical dome girth-welded to each end of the body shell

parts.name: machined_boss
parts.role: polar boss (outlet 19 mm ID, inlet 12 mm ID) welded into the center of each end cap

parts.name: skirt_flange
parts.role: 4-point mounting skirt flange brazed at the cylindrical girth; outside the pressure boundary

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Fty at 1.5 x MDP proof per ECSS Table 6-2
Ftu * E_w = 950 * 0.85 in weld; 950 in parent; burst = 2.0 x MDP
a_crit / t_wall must be >=1; K_I = 1.12*sigma*sqrt(pi*a) >= K_IC
Paris law a_i = 0.3 mm -> a_crit, C=1e-11, m=3.5

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: axisymmetric or 3D TET10 at assembly level with weld-zone tagging

interfaces.load_path: meridional + hoop membrane stress at pressure, with weld stress concentration
local reinforcement around penetration for pressure stress
launch inertial load into spacecraft bus (boundary condition)

fem_check_policy.rationale: Weld efficiency factor E_w = 0.85 only applies at the girth welds that exist because the body shell, end caps, and bosses are separate parts. Leak-before-burst and cyclic fatigue checks depend on the wall + weld stress state of the integrated shell.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: A spun-and-welded pressure vessel cannot be fabricated as a single monolithic body; the cylindrical body, hemispherical caps, and bosses are distinct fabricated parts joined by girth welds whose efficiency factor explicitly appears in the pass/fail.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Launch environment rolled into LC3 quasi-static.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Temperature band imposed as bulk delta; no transient.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Propellant is lumped as mass; slosh out of scope.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Internal pressure stabilizes the shell; external-collapse buckling is not a design driver.

eval_coverage: proof + burst + launch combined-load checks on welded assembly
leak-before-burst fracture mechanics with weld-zone stress concentration
weld efficiency factor applied only to weld region
